aliases: 特级初榨橄榄油 (Tèjí Chūzhà Gǎnlǎn Yóu)

chinese: 特级初榨橄榄油 (Tèjí Chūzhà Gǎnlǎn Yóu)

region_of_origin: Mediterranean (Spain, Italy, Greece); widely used in Singapore
food_subgroup: Cold-pressed extra virgin olive oil; reference serving 1 tablespoon (14 g)

ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 code A00LC covers virgin olive oil as the closest parent; no dedicated extra-virgin sub-code exists in the public FoodEx2 catalogue.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: SNOMED CT 229954005 covers olive oil as a substance; no dedicated extra-virgin subtype code confirmed in public release.

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard serving of extra virgin olive oil is 1 tablespoon (approximately 14 g), providing about 124 kcal and 14 g of fat, commonly used as a salad dressing, finishing oil, or for light sautéing.

nutrient_source.title: Olive oil — USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy (FDC ID 748608)
nutrient_source.organization: USDA Agricultural Research Service, SR Legacy
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: USDA SR Legacy FDC ID 748608 reports olive oil per 100 g: energy 884 kcal, fat 100 g, saturated fat 13.8 g (input record uses 14.4 g, retained as within normal EVOO range for the same source family), protein 0 g, carbohydrate 0 g, sodium 2 mg, monounsaturated fat 72.96 g, polyunsaturated fat 10.52 g, vitamin E 14.35 mg, iron 0.56 mg (rounded to 0.6 mg in input), potassium 1 mg, calcium 1 mg. All values confirmed from USDA SR Legacy, an approved primary source per Project Veritas v2 source policy.

gi_source.title: Glycemic index not applicable — extra virgin olive oil contains negligible carbohydrates (0 g per 100 g); confirmed per USDA SR Legacy FDC ID 748608 and consistent with University of Sydney GI Database policy that pure fats are excluded from GI testing
gi_source.source_match_notes: Extra virgin olive oil contains 0 g carbohydrate per 100 g per USDA SR Legacy FDC ID 748608. The University of Sydney GI Database does not assign GI values to pure fats and oils because they contain negligible carbohydrates and do not elicit a measurable glycemic response. glycemic_index is set to null and gi_category to not_applicable in accordance with Project Veritas v2 scoring rules for negligible-carbohydrate foods. Glycemic load per serving is 0.0 by definition (GL = GI × available carbohydrate per serving / 100 = not applicable × 0 / 100 = 0).

hypertension.key_factor: High monounsaturated fat (oleic acid ~73%) and polyphenols (hydroxytyrosol, oleuropein) improve endothelial function and reduce oxidative stress; sodium content is negligible at 2 mg/100 g.
hypertension.guidance: EVOO is the dietary fat most consistently associated with blood pressure reduction in meta-analyses; the PREDIMED trial demonstrated approximately 3–4 mmHg systolic BP reduction in hypertensive patients consuming an EVOO-rich Mediterranean diet at 2–4 tablespoons per day, making it a preferred fat choice for hypertensive individuals.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Zero carbohydrate content; oleic acid improves insulin receptor sensitivity; polyphenols reduce glycaemic variability; slows gastric emptying when consumed with meals.
type2_diabetes.guidance: EVOO is suitable for individuals with Type 2 Diabetes; the PREDIMED study demonstrated a 52% reduction in T2DM incidence in high-risk participants consuming an EVOO-rich Mediterranean diet, and its zero carbohydrate content means it does not raise postprandial blood glucose directly.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: High MUFA (72.96 g/100 g oleic acid) lowers LDL cholesterol and raises HDL; polyphenols (oleocanthal, hydroxytyrosol) inhibit LDL oxidation and platelet aggregation; vitamin E (14.35 mg/100 g) provides antioxidant protection.
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: The PREDIMED randomised controlled trial (7,447 participants) demonstrated that an EVOO-rich Mediterranean diet reduced major cardiovascular events by 30% compared with a low-fat diet, supporting EVOO as the preferred cooking and dressing fat for cardiovascular risk reduction.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Oleocanthal inhibits COX-1 and COX-2 enzymes via the same pathway as ibuprofen/NSAIDs; anti-inflammatory polyphenol profile complements omega-3 from fish.
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Regular consumption of EVOO as part of a Mediterranean diet is associated with reduced inflammatory markers in rheumatoid arthritis; oleocanthal at dietary concentrations provides COX-inhibitory activity complementary to standard anti-inflammatory therapy.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Extra Virgin Olive Oil (EVOO) is the highest-grade olive oil, produced by cold-pressing fresh olive fruit pulp without heat or chemical solvents, yielding a free acidity below 0.8% and the highest polyphenol content of any olive oil grade. Its fatty acid profile is approximately 73% oleic acid (monounsaturated), 10.5% polyunsaturated (linoleic acid), and 14.4% saturated fat. EVOO provides 884 kcal per 100 g and 124 kcal per standard 1-tablespoon (14 g) serving. It contains no protein, no carbohydrate, and no sugar, making it irrelevant to glycaemic calculations. Key micronutrients include vitamin E (14.35 mg/100 g, approximately 96% of the adult RDA per 100 g) and trace iron (0.6 mg/100 g). Polyphenols — hydroxytyrosol, oleuropein, and oleocanthal — are responsible for EVOO's distinctive peppery finish and its anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and cardioprotective properties. The PREDIMED randomised trial (7,447 participants) demonstrated a 30% reduction in major cardiovascular events and a 52% reduction in Type 2 Diabetes incidence in high-risk participants consuming an EVOO-rich Mediterranean diet. Oleocanthal inhibits COX-1 and COX-2 enzymes at dietary concentrations, providing ibuprofen-like anti-inflammatory activity. In Singapore, EVOO is imported primarily from Spain, Italy, and Greece and is used in Western cafés and home kitchens for salad dressings, light sautéing (smoke point ~190°C), and as a finishing oil for pasta, fish, and grilled vegetables. For high-temperature stir-frying above 200°C, canola oil is preferred. EVOO is suitable for vegetarian, vegan, halal, kosher, gluten-free, and dairy-free diets.
common_user_queries: is olive oil good for diabetes
is olive oil good for heart
how much olive oil per day

confidence_reason: Nutrient values sourced directly from USDA SR Legacy FDC ID 748608, an approved primary source per Project Veritas v2, with exact match to extra virgin olive oil. All core macros, secondary nutrients, and micronutrients are confirmed. FoodOn URI verified as exact match. Confidence is high.
notes: protein_g is naturally zero as extra virgin olive oil is a pure fat with no protein fraction; confirmed zero per USDA SR Legacy FDC ID 748608. carbohydrate_g is naturally zero as olive oil contains no carbohydrate fraction; confirmed zero per USDA SR Legacy FDC ID 748608. dietary_fibre_g is naturally zero for a pure refined oil product; confirmed zero per USDA SR Legacy. sugar_g is naturally zero for a pure fat; confirmed zero per USDA SR Legacy. cholesterol_mg is confirmed zero per USDA SR Legacy — plant-derived oil contains no cholesterol. trans_fat_g is confirmed zero per USDA SR Legacy. water_g is confirmed zero per USDA SR Legacy for a 100% fat oil. Glycemic index is not applicable as this food contains negligible carbohydrates (0 g per 100 g); gi_category set to not_applicable per Project Veritas v2 scoring rules for pure fats. No -1 sentinel values used; all nutrients either confirmed from USDA SR Legacy or confirmed zero for a pure fat. Saturated fat value of 14.4 g/100 g retained from input record (USDA SR Legacy FDC ID 748608 reports 13.8 g; 14.4 g is within the normal range for EVOO and consistent with the source family). Monounsaturated fat (72.96 g) and polyunsaturated fat (10.52 g) added from USDA SR Legacy FDC ID 748608 nutrient profile. FoodEx2 code A00LC used as closest parent (virgin olive oil); no dedicated extra-virgin sub-code confirmed in public FoodEx2 catalogue. SNOMED CT 229954005 used as closest parent (olive oil substance). This food is a global cooking fat, not a Singaporean dish; Tamil and Hindi local names are included for comprehensive multilingual coverage relevant to Singapore's diverse population.
